text: Writing clean code is essential for any developer. The most important quality of clean code is that it is readable for both yourself and others. This starts with using meaningful naming conventions; for example, a variable called `userAge` is much clearer than `ua`. Secondly, it is good practice to break your code into small, focused functions. Each function should do just one thing. This makes your code easier to understand, test, and reuse. Ultimately, these habits greatly improve the maintainability of your project. When you return to your code after several months, or when a colleague needs to update it, clean code saves time and prevents confusion.
